Through the Spectrum: A Circle of Light and Perception
PREVIEW
HISTORY
COMMENTS
TASKS
COPY
ASSISTANTS
IMAGE
VIDEO
High above the rooftops of Aarhus, a luminous circle hovers like a quiet horizon. It is here,
on top of the ARoS Art Museum, that Olafur Eliasson has inscribed his Your rainbow
panorama into the city’s skyline, an artwork that is at once architecture, observation deck,
and sensory experiment.
Olafur Eliassons Your rainbow panorama på taget af ARoS
Eliasson, the Danish-Icelandic artist known for exploring light, perception, and the
relationship between humans and their environment, conceived the installation as a
walkable spectrum. A circular corridor of glass unfolds in every color of the rainbow, inviting
visitors to step inside and move through shifting atmospheres of red, green, blue, and
everything in between. With each step, the city below transforms: streets dissolve into tinted
abstractions, familiar landmarks reappear as if filtered through emotion rather than optics.
What might first appear as a striking visual landmark reveals itself, on closer encounter, as a
subtle investigation into how we see. The panorama does not simply frame Aarhus, it alters
it. Visitors become participants in an ongoing interplay between body, space, and
perception. The act of walking becomes a form of looking, and looking becomes a process
shaped by color, movement, and awareness.
